repository_type: Multi-omics portal; public archive; controlled-access archive
omics_layer: Multi-omics; Bulk transcriptomics; Metagenomics; Metatranscriptomics; Metabolomics; Metaproteomics; Virome
sample_size_samples: Unclear (hundreds to thousands across omics layers)
potential_use_cases: Host-microbiome integration; longitudinal disease activity; integrative multi-omics; treatment response; remission prediction
limitations: Complex multi-omics structure; mixed access levels; harmonization required across modalities; visit-level completeness varies across participants
data_access_status: Public processed data; Controlled access (some components)
notes: Core HMP2 (Human Microbiome Project Phase 2) study. Includes host transcriptomics, metagenomics, metatranscriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics, and virome components. Some raw data components require dbGaP approval (phs001559).

potential_use_cases: Curated IBD transcriptomic comparisons; dataset discovery; cross-study meta-analysis support
limitations: Aggregated resource; individual study metadata may be incomplete; platform harmonization varies; requires verification of current availability
notes: Curated database aggregating published IBD transcriptomic studies. Current URL and access status need manual verification.

potential_use_cases: Mucosal transcriptomics; disease-vs-control comparison; CD vs UC comparison
limitations: Metadata completeness uncertain; population characteristics need verification
notes: GEO accession for IBD mucosal bulk RNA-seq. Full metadata should be verified against GEO record.

potential_use_cases: Mucosal disease-vs-control comparison; bulk transcriptomic analysis; CD vs UC colon transcriptomics
limitations: Metadata completeness uncertain; needs verification
notes: GEO accession for IBD colon RNA-seq. Full metadata should be verified.

potential_use_cases: Pediatric IBD biology; treatment-naive mucosal signatures; pediatric CD ileal expression
limitations: Pediatric only; microarray platform; treatment naivety status should be verified
notes: GEO accession for pediatric IBD ileal microarray. Often cited as a treatment-naive pediatric cohort.

potential_use_cases: Pediatric mucosal IBD expression; pediatric colon transcriptomics
limitations: Pediatric only; microarray platform; metadata completeness uncertain
notes: GEO accession for pediatric IBD colon microarray.

potential_use_cases: Paired tissue/blood biomarker analysis; pediatric IBD biomarkers; mucosal-blood correlation
limitations: Pediatric only; paired design may limit sample sizes per group
notes: Paired colon biopsy and whole blood mRNA profiling in pediatric IBD.

potential_use_cases: Mucosal CD-vs-UC-vs-control comparison; colonic transcriptomics
limitations: Microarray platform; metadata needs verification
notes: GEO accession for IBD colonic microarray with CD, UC, and control comparisons.

disease: Active UC; inactive UC; active CD; inactive CD; infectious colitis; controls
potential_use_cases: Disease activity signatures; differential diagnosis; mucosal inflammation; UC vs CD vs infectious colitis
limitations: Older dataset; microarray platform; disease activity classification may not use current indices
notes: Includes endoscopic biopsies across active/inactive disease states and infectious colitis.

potential_use_cases: CD-vs-UC mucosal comparison; sigmoid colon signatures
limitations: Older dataset; limited disease activity metadata; microarray platform
notes: Early IBD microarray comparing CD and UC sigmoid colon biopsies.

potential_use_cases: Mucosal IBD-control comparison; intestinal transcriptomics
limitations: Metadata completeness uncertain; needs verification
notes: GEO accession for IBD intestinal mucosal microarray.

potential_use_cases: Blood-based IBD transcriptomics; systemic expression signatures; CD vs UC blood comparison
limitations: Blood-based; older microarray dataset; confounding by cell composition
notes: Peripheral blood microarray comparing CD, UC, and controls.

disease: Active UC; inactive UC; active CD; inactive CD; controls
potential_use_cases: Systemic activity signatures; blood-based disease activity; CD vs UC blood comparison
limitations: Blood-based; cell composition confounding; disease activity classification should be verified
notes: Whole blood microarray across active and inactive disease states in UC and CD.

potential_use_cases: Mucosal IBD meta-analysis; colonic expression signatures
limitations: Disease group composition should be verified against GEO metadata
notes: Colonic mucosal microarray. Disease groups should be confirmed in GEO record.

potential_use_cases: UC mucosal transcriptomics; disease-vs-control comparison
limitations: Platform and disease group composition should be verified
notes: GEO accession for UC colonic mucosal transcriptomics.

potential_use_cases: UC mucosal transcriptomics; disease-vs-control comparison
limitations: Platform and disease group composition should be verified
notes: GEO accession for UC colonic mucosal transcriptomics.

potential_use_cases: Cell-type/state reference; single-cell disease atlas; deconvolution support; immune cell profiling
limitations: Single-cell data requires specialized analysis; cell annotation may vary
notes: scRNA-seq of colonic cells in UC, CD, and healthy controls.

potential_use_cases: Spatial localization of inflammatory cell states; spatial IBD atlas; tissue architecture
limitations: Spatial transcriptomics requires specialized analysis; resolution and sensitivity differ from scRNA-seq
notes: CosMx spatial transcriptomics of human inflamed and uninflamed intestine.

potential_use_cases: Treatment-response cellular trajectories; anti-TNF response; longitudinal single-cell atlas; remission cellular signatures
limitations: Treatment-specific cohort; longitudinal design limits cross-study comparisons
notes: Longitudinal scRNA-seq treatment atlas focusing on anti-TNF/adalimumab response.

dataset_name: Martin et al. 2019 Cell / GIMATS (anti-TNF resistance)
publication_title: Fibroblasts and Pericytes Immunoregulation in IBD Lesions (approximate; needs verification)
potential_use_cases: Anti-TNF resistance biology; cell-state modules; inflammatory fibroblast signatures; ileal Crohn's disease cell atlas
limitations: Accession needs verification; ileal CD only; limited disease comparators
notes: GIMATS resource from Martin et al. Cell 2019 describing fibroblast/stromal cell states in anti-TNF-resistant CD. Accession number needs manual verification.

potential_use_cases: Cell atlas reference; deconvolution reference; cell-state interpretation; marker validation; bulk deconvolution
limitations: Heterogeneous studies; annotation standards may vary; access routes may differ across datasets
notes: Human Cell Atlas IBD-related datasets including terminal ileum and colon. Accessible through HCA portal and CELLxGENE.

potential_use_cases: Public exploration and download of annotated IBD single-cell data; multi-study comparison; cell-type reference
limitations: Multiple studies with different annotations; harmonization required for cross-study analysis
notes: CELLxGENE hosts multiple annotated IBD scRNA-seq datasets with standardized cell ontology annotations.

potential_use_cases: Non-invasive fecal protein biomarkers; IBD diagnosis; fecal proteomic atlas
limitations: Fecal proteomics susceptible to dietary and microbiome variability; DIA-specific analysis pipelines required
notes: Fecal DIA-MS proteomics in IBD and healthy controls. Deposited in PRIDE and MassIVE.

potential_use_cases: Diagnostic metaproteomics; host-microbiome protein detection; fecal biomarker discovery
limitations: Mixed host and microbial proteins; requires dedicated metaproteomics search databases
notes: Fecal metaproteomics LC-MS/MS in IBD and healthy controls.

potential_use_cases: Mucosal proteomics; UC-specific protein expression; mucosal protein biomarkers
limitations: Tissue proteomics requires careful sample handling; older dataset
notes: LC-MS/MS proteomics of human colon biopsies in UC and controls.

potential_use_cases: Integrated stool proteome-metabolome analysis; multi-omics stool biomarkers; joint IBD biomarker discovery
limitations: Requires multi-omics integration methodology; fecal variability
notes: Combined fecal proteomics and metabolomics dataset in IBD and controls.

potential_use_cases: UC metabolomics; disease severity; treatment response; metabolic signatures
limitations: Sample type and metadata completeness need verification
notes: UC metabolomics LC-MS study. Metadata and sample type should be verified against Metabolomics Workbench record.

potential_use_cases: Systemic methylation signatures; epigenetic biomarkers; IBD blood EWAS
limitations: Cell composition confounding in blood methylation; platform-specific issues
notes: EWAS of newly diagnosed IBD cases and controls using blood methylation.

potential_use_cases: Blood methylation comparison; CD vs UC epigenomics; peripheral epigenetic signatures
limitations: Cell composition confounding; 450K array coverage
notes: Illumina 450K methylation array in peripheral blood from CD, UC, and controls.

potential_use_cases: Mucosal methylation; CD vs UC colon epigenomics; tissue-level epigenetic differences
limitations: 450K array coverage; tissue site and disease activity should be verified
notes: Illumina 450K methylation array in colon mucosa from CD, UC, and controls.

potential_use_cases: Mucosal methylation validation; colon epigenomics replication
limitations: 450K array; validation cohort design may differ from discovery
notes: Illumina 450K methylation array in colon mucosa — often used as a validation cohort alongside GSE32146.

potential_use_cases: Epithelial epigenetic mechanisms in IBD; intestinal epithelial cell epigenomics; organoid epigenomics
limitations: Platform and exact sample type need verification; limited to epithelial compartment
notes: Epigenetic and/or transcriptomic resource from intestinal epithelial/organoid context in IBD. Details need verification.

potential_use_cases: Genetic risk factors; target prioritization; Mendelian randomization; colocalization; fine-mapping; trans-ancestral analysis
limitations: Controlled-access individual-level data; GWAS summary statistics are public; ancestry representation varies
data_access_status: Public processed data; Controlled access (individual data)
notes: International consortium for IBD GWAS. Summary statistics are publicly accessible; individual-level data require dbGaP approval.

potential_use_cases: Published GWAS lookup; trait mapping; locus discovery; genetic correlation; PRS development
limitations: Summary statistics only; different studies use different imputation panels; requires harmonization for meta-analysis
notes: Aggregated IBD/CD/UC GWAS results in the GWAS Catalog. Includes summary statistics from multiple published studies.

potential_use_cases: Controlled-access IIBDGC analyses; fine-mapping; trans-ancestral GWAS; individual-level analysis
limitations: Requires dbGaP application and approval; individual-level data only accessible after approval
notes: IIBDGC individual-level genotype data in dbGaP. Requires application and institutional approval.

potential_use_cases: Target prioritization; genetics-derived IBD target evidence; locus-to-gene mapping; drug target identification
limitations: Not a primary dataset; derived summaries only; underlying data sources should be verified
notes: Open Targets integrates GWAS and functional genomics data to support IBD target prioritization. Not a raw dataset.

dataset_name: FinnGen / UK Biobank IBD GWAS Resources
repository: FinnGen; UK Biobank; Open GWAS (MRC IEU)
disease: IBD; CD; UC (phenotypes vary by resource)
platform: SNP array; whole genome sequencing; imputed
potential_use_cases: External genetic validation; Mendelian randomization; colocalization; phenotype comparison; ancestry diversity
limitations: IBD phenotype ascertainment varies (ICD codes vs clinical records); UK Biobank individual data require access application; ancestry representation limited in FinnGen
data_access_status: Public processed data; Controlled access (individual data)
notes: FinnGen (Finnish biobank) and UK Biobank provide large-scale GWAS summary statistics accessible for IBD-related analyses. Summary statistics available via Open GWAS.
